segments.text: This is Brock. Rock likes rocks. Rocks, theoretically, should be really good in the early game of
Pokemon Crystal, so it caused me to do a little bit of a challenge. I want to see how good the
original Gen 1 Gym Leader would have been if he had been able to use his Rock-type Pokemon in
Gen 2. Could he beat the first Gym? Could he beat maybe even the second Gym? Could he even get an
advantage on the third gym. I just want to find out how far a Gen 1 Brock could actually get in
this game. Now Brock is very interesting because when he comes over here to meet Professor Elm,
he's going to help conduct a little bit of research on this kind of cross-gen challenge,
which means that he's going to get a very special setup from Professor Elm. You see,
Professor Elm has been playing Pokemon Red and Blue quite a bit, and he decided to put an Onix
inside of this ball, but when we pick up this Onix, we're actually also going to get a Geodude,
because why wouldn't we get a Geodude? It just makes perfect sense. And just like that,
we have Brock's exact team, a level 14 Onix and a level 12 Geodude with the exact same moves and
stats that they would have in Pokemon Red and Blue. And our goal is simple, to find out how far
Brock could actually get in Pokemon Crystal, no experience, just his two Pokemon using the exact
moves that they would normally have. Now, one of the things that makes Brock very special is that
he's obsessed with full heals, which means he can use five full heals per Pokemon in every single
battle. Now, just like my other gym leader challenges here in Gen 2, I'm going to be
running these Pokemon with no experience, and we're simply going to find out how far they can
actually get in the game. We did Lorelai last time and found that she could basically beat
everything until she got really late into Kanto. Of course, observing some basic aspects of her AI,
Brock simply attacks randomly. So in theory, we're going to have to randomize between our moves a
little bit. We might have to use defense girl even when it's not really effective to use defense girl
spam bite in situations where we wouldn't really want to. But I think all of that is perfectly
fine. We're just going to test this and see how far Brock could go. Now, Brock is a Gen 1 Gym
Leader, which means he has no idea how these berries work, so we're simply going to take the
berry and throw it on the ground. Same for this potion. Brock doesn't use potions, so that gets
thrown on the ground as well. What Brock does like to use, though, is full heals. Gen 1 Brock would
have five full heals per Pokemon in every single battle, so it just makes sense to give him an
infinite number of full heals. I've just got it locked in and game hooked so I can just keep
using them over and over and over again, up to five times per battle, and he'll just use it
immediately anytime he takes any sort of status. Outside of that though, Brock is a pretty
straightforward guy. He just likes to use his rock Pokemon in attack, and he's not smart enough to
ever switch Pokemon out or do anything like that. So all he's going to do is lead off with Geodude
a hundred percent of the time, and then when Geodude gets knocked out, he'll then swap to his
Onix. Now, because we are a bit overleveled here, I don't expect to have any trouble with Rival
number one, but let's just see how this fight actually goes. So Rival one, of course, has the
Chikorita, arguably the hardest rival for us to actually get through, but we're just going to go
ahead and use Tackle on this one, and we can see we do massive damage. Theoretically, we would mix
with tackle and defense girl so i'll use a defense girl just randomly because i can we even miss a
tackle good job brocky boy but once we manage to get a few tackles off we are going to win this
fight and it's not like the chikorita has any real shot of defeating our geo dude so we easily
defeat rival number one and i basically predict we're going to do pretty well in this until at
least bugsy maybe fury cutter builds up so much that we just get completely wrecked there because
Brock in Gen 1 doesn't have any Brock-type moves, which is something that I accuse Game Freak of,

in Gen 1, and even when they did, they made them, like, trash moves, like the 65% accurate Rock

text: This is Brock. Rock likes rocks. Rocks, theoretically, should be really good in the early game of Pokemon Crystal, so it caused me to do a little bit of a challenge. I want to see how good the original Gen 1 Gym Leader would have been if he had been able to use his Rock-type Pokemon in Gen 2. Could he beat the first Gym? Could he beat maybe even the second Gym? Could he even get an advantage on the third gym. I just want to find out how far a Gen 1 Brock could actually get in this game. Now Brock is very interesting because when he comes over here to meet Professor Elm, he's going to help conduct a little bit of research on this kind of cross-gen challenge, which means that he's going to get a very special setup from Professor Elm. You see, Professor Elm has been playing Pokemon Red and Blue quite a bit, and he decided to put an Onix inside of this ball, but when we pick up this Onix, we're actually also going to get a Geodude, because why wouldn't we get a Geodude? It just makes perfect sense. And just like that, we have Brock's exact team, a level 14 Onix and a level 12 Geodude with the exact same moves and stats that they would have in Pokemon Red and Blue. And our goal is simple, to find out how far Brock could actually get in Pokemon Crystal, no experience, just his two Pokemon using the exact moves that they would normally have. Now, one of the things that makes Brock very special is that he's obsessed with full heals, which means he can use five full heals per Pokemon in every single battle. Now, just like my other gym leader challenges here in Gen 2, I'm going to be running these Pokemon with no experience, and we're simply going to find out how far they can actually get in the game. We did Lorelai last time and found that she could basically beat everything until she got really late into Kanto. Of course, observing some basic aspects of her AI, Brock simply attacks randomly. So in theory, we're going to have to randomize between our moves a little bit. We might have to use defense girl even when it's not really effective to use defense girl spam bite in situations where we wouldn't really want to. But I think all of that is perfectly fine.
